extraction.one_line_summary: SCHACHMANN Max won from reduced group after ALAPHILIPPE Julian's 50km breakaway attempt with SOLER exhausted him before final kilometer
tactical_patterns: Alaphilippe attacked at intermediate sprint with Soler, bridged 20+ seconds gap, but burned matches before finale allowing Schachmann win.
Seven absent teams created coordination vacuum enabling persistent splits from 50km out; EF/Trek forced prolonged chase without domestiques.
Bora delivered Schachmann to front group despite Sagan weakness, showing GC rider prioritization over depleted sprint train.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: burned himself attacking 30km out for bonus seconds, had nothing left when group caught him in final kilometer
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Max: positive: positioned perfectly on Teunissen wheel with 150m out, suggesting superior race reading when strongest riders emptied
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: explicitly noted as not looking himself, Bora ignored him entirely for Schachmann GC ambitions
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: caught out in split at 50km despite predictable wind/terrain, poor positioning for supposed GC contender
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: also caught in 50km split alongside Porte, raises questions about Trek's front-of-race awareness
